title: Polysaccharide Hydrolase Activities in Danish Coastal Seawater and Sediments under Varying Hydrostatic Pressures
author: Lloyd, C. Chad
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The potential of the seawater or sedimentary microbial community to hydrolyze seven high-molecular-weight polysaccharides (arabinogalactan, chondroitin sulfate, fucoidan, laminarin, mannan, pullulan, and xylan) was investigated in a coastal station off the coast of Helsingor, Denmark. This investigation was part of the larger project to understand pressure effects on enzymatic activity and these samples were collected in September, 2023.
steps_raw: The potential of the seawater or sedimentary microbial community to hydrolyze seven high-molecular-weight polysaccharides (arabinogalactan, chondroitin sulfate, fucoidan, laminarin, mannan, pullulan, and xylan) was investigated in a coastal station off the coast of Helsingor, Denmark. This investigation was part of the larger project to understand pressure effects on enzymatic activity and these samples were collected in September, 2023.
